title: helianthemum scoparium var. aldersonii
intro: Helianthemum scoparium var. aldersonii, commonly known as New Mexico rockrose or Alderson's rockrose, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Cistaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, colds, and wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that provide several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Helianthemum scoparium var. aldersonii is often prepared as a tea or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties and can also be used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
